records.ety: [OE. cracken, craken, to crack, break, boast, AS. cracian, cearcian, to crack; akin to D. kraken, G. krachen; cf. Skr. garj to rattle, or perh. of imitative origin. Cf. Crake, Cracknel, Creak.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Cracked (krkt); p. pr. & vb. n. Cracking.]

defs: To break or burst, with or without entire separation of the parts; as, to crack glass; to crack nuts.
To rend with grief or pain; to affect deeply with sorrow; hence, to disorder; to distract; to craze.
quotes: O, madam, my old heart is cracked.
He thought none poets till their brains were cracked.

defs: To cause to sound suddenly and sharply; to snap; as, to crack a whip.
To utter smartly and sententiously; as, to crack a joke.
To cry up; to extol; -- followed by up.
To burst or open in chinks; to break, with or without quite separating into parts.
quotes: By misfortune it cracked in the coling.
The mirror cracked from side to side.

defs: To be ruined or impaired; to fail.
quotes: The credit . . . of exchequers cracks, when little comes in and much goes out.

defs: To utter a loud or sharp, sudden sound.
quotes: As thunder when the clouds in autumn crack.

defs: To utter vain, pompous words; to brag; to boast; -- with of.
quotes: Ethoipes of their sweet complexion crack.

defs: A partial separation of parts, with or without a perceptible opening; a chink or fissure; a narrow breach; a crevice; as, a crack in timber, or in a wall, or in glass.
Rupture; flaw; breach, in a moral sense.
quotes: My love to thee is sound, sans crack or flaw.

defs: A sharp, sudden sound or report; the sound of anything suddenly burst or broken; as, the crack of a falling house; the crack of thunder; the crack of a whip.
quotes: Will the stretch out to the crack of doom?

defs: The tone of voice when changed at puberty.
quotes: Though now our voices Have got the mannish crack.

defs: Mental flaw; a touch of craziness; partial insanity; as, he has a crack.
A crazy or crack-brained person.
quotes: I . . . can not get the Parliament to listen to me, who look upon me as a crack and a projector.

defs: A boast; boasting.
Breach of chastity.
A boy, generally a pert, lively boy.
quotes: Val. 'T is a noble child. Vir. A crack, madam.

defs: A brief time; an instant; as, to be with one in a crack.
Free conversation; friendly chat.
quotes: What is crack in English? . . . A crack is . . . a chat with a good, kindly human heart in it.

defs: a witty remark; a wisecrack.
a chance or opportunity to do something; an attempt; as, I'll take a crack at it.
a form of cocaine, highly purified and prepared as small pellets, especially suitable for smoking; -- also called rock. Used in this form it appears to be more addicting than cocaine powder.
Of superior excellence; having qualities to be boasted of; as, a crack shot.
quotes: One of our crack speakers in the Commons.
